title: Community Relations: Nonschool Use of School Facilities
description: Conroe ISD's GKD policy governs how school district facilities may be used by nonschool groups and individuals. It establishes categories of facility users, sets a priority system for scheduling, and outlines fee structures for different types of users. The policy also addresses requirements for facility use and permits religious organizations to use district facilities for worship and related activities under standard terms.

timeline_local.summary: This is a comprehensive rewrite of Conroe ISD's GKD (Nonschool Use of School Facilities) local policy. The update introduces new defined categories of facility users (District Facility, Facility User, Campus Affiliated Facility User, Employee Facility User, Student Activity, Student After School Facility User), replaces the old paper-based application process with an online portal system, restructures scheduling priorities into a formal numbered hierarchy, overhauls fee structures with detailed provisions for different user categories, and replaces the old "Special Provisions" and "Required Conduct" sections with a new comprehensive "Facility Use Requirements" section, while adding a transition deadline of August 31, 2026.

elements.element: Creation of new defined user categories (District Facility, Facility User, Campus Affiliated Facility User, Employee Facility User, Student Activity, Student After School Facility User)
elements.supporting_quote: The trustees may adopt rules and bylaws necessary to carry out the powers and duties provided by Subsection (b).
elements.local_additions: The statute authorizes general rulemaking but contains no requirement or express permission to define facility-user categories. The specific taxonomy of six user categories is entirely the district's own structural choice with no statutory analog.

elements.element: Replacement of paper-based application process with an online portal system
elements.local_additions: No statute cited in the analysis requires or expressly authorizes an online portal over any other application mechanism. The choice of an online portal is a purely operational and administrative decision by Conroe ISD with no direct statutory basis.

elements.element: Formal numbered scheduling priority hierarchy for facility use
elements.supporting_quote: The trustees may adopt rules and bylaws necessary to carry out the powers and duties provided by Subsection (b).
elements.local_additions: While TEC §11.151 broadly permits the board to adopt rules governing district property management, no statute requires or specifically authorizes a tiered priority structure for scheduling. The numbered hierarchy, its specific ordering, and which user categories receive priority are entirely local design choices.

elements.element: Overhauled fee structures with detailed provisions differentiated by user category
elements.supporting_quote: receive bequests and donations or other moneys or funds coming legally into their hands
elements.local_additions: TEC §11.151(a) permits the board to receive funds lawfully obtained, providing background authority to charge fees for facility use. However, no statute in the analysis requires or expressly authorizes a specific fee schedule, fee differentiation by user category, or any particular fee structure. The detailed fee framework is a district-originated policy choice.

elements.element: Replacement of 'Special Provisions' and 'Required Conduct' sections with new comprehensive 'Facility Use Requirements' section
elements.supporting_quote: The trustees may adopt rules and bylaws necessary to carry out the powers and duties provided by Subsection (b).
elements.local_additions: The restructuring and consolidation of conduct and operational requirements into a new section is a drafting and organizational choice by the district. No statute in the analysis mandates the content, format, or existence of a 'Facility Use Requirements' section. The board's general rulemaking authority under TEC §11.151(c) provides background governance authority, but all substantive content choices are locally discretionary.

No statute cited in the analysis requires or authorizes any specific implementation deadline. The August 31, 2026 transition date is entirely a district-selected administrative timeline with no statutory compulsion.

elements.element: Overall board authority to govern nonschool use of school facilities
elements.supporting_quote: the trustees as a body corporate have the exclusive power and duty to govern and oversee the management of the public schools of the district
elements.local_additions: TEC §11.151 broadly vests governance and management of district property in the board and permits rulemaking, but no statute in the analysis specifically requires the district to adopt or maintain a facility use policy at all. The decision to have such a policy and to comprehensively rewrite it is the district's own exercise of permissive board authority. TEC §11.1511 adds authority to enter into contracts and delegate superintendent authority, providing further permissive grounding. No mandatory language compels a facility use policy or any particular rewrite.

compliance_summary: Board Authority Over Facilities: Texas Education Code §11.151 gives school boards broad authority to manage district property and adopt rules about its use. This policy rewrite is the district's own choice to exercise that authority — no state law required it to update or maintain a facility use policy.

New User Categories: The updated policy creates six defined categories for people and groups who want to use school facilities, such as campus-affiliated users and employee facility users. State law does not require or describe these categories; the district designed this classification system on its own.

Online Application Portal: The policy replaces paper applications with an online portal for requesting facility use. No state law requires or specifically allows an online system over any other method — this is an administrative choice made entirely by the district.

Scheduling Priority System: The policy sets up a numbered priority list that determines which users get first access to facilities when there is scheduling competition. Texas Education Code §11.151 gives the board general authority to manage district property, but the specific ranking order and structure were designed by the district without any state requirement to do so.

Fee Structure by User Type: The policy establishes a detailed fee schedule that charges different amounts depending on which user category is requesting the facility. Texas Education Code §11.151(a) permits the board to receive funds lawfully obtained, but the specific fees and how they differ by user type are decisions made entirely by the district.

Transition Deadline: The policy sets August 31, 2026 as the deadline for moving to the new system. No state law requires this date or any specific implementation timeline — it is a scheduling decision made by the district.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's GKD (Local) policy adds a new "Religious Organization Use" section that explicitly permits religious organizations to use district facilities for worship, services, sermons, or assemblies, subject to standard terms and fees (unless waived by the Board), and holds them liable for any damages incurred during use.
changes_added: Religious Organization Use section (new heading and full text): 'The District may allow a religious organization to use the district's or school's facilities to host religious worship, services, sermons, or assemblies only if the facility use does not interfere with the District's educational mission (see GKD Legal for clarification). A religious organization shall follow the District's standard terms and pay the District's standard fees for usage, unless the Board waives the fees. A religious organization shall be liable for any damages while utilizing district facilities.'
timeline_local.notes: The only redlined (red underlined) text appears on page 2 under the new "Religious Organization Use" heading and its accompanying paragraph. All other policy text is in black (unchanged). No red strikethrough/removal text was detected. The cover page title reads "Discuss Revisions to Board Policy GKD (Local)," indicating a first reading.

description_local: This policy governs how school buildings, grounds, and other facilities can be used by outside groups and organizations when school is not in session. It applies to community members, local organizations, and groups — including religious organizations — who may want to rent or borrow school spaces for meetings, events, or activities. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it sets the rules for who can access school facilities, under what conditions, and on what terms, helping ensure that public school property is used fairly and appropriately by the broader community.
